item.text:
Wind and waves can make the ship move in six different ways, for example: 

These are the angular motions: 
The ship is |rolling*pitching*yawing| when she moves from her starboard side to her port side with the waves. 
The ship is |pitching*yawing*heaving| when her bow and her stern rise and fall with the waves. 
The ship is |yawing*heaving*surging| when her bow is pushed to starboard and then to port. 

These are the linear motions: 
The ship is |heaving*surging*swaying| when she rises up and falls back into a trough. 
The ship is |surging*swaying*rolling| when she is pushed forward by the waves and then falls back into a trough. 
The ship is |swaying*rolling*pitching| when she moves bodily to starboard and then to port.
